title: Super Cappuccino: Scam or Legitimate Morph?
description: The community calls super cappuccino a scam. The genetics say it should work. Here's the real cost—financial and ethical—of breeding Cappuccino × Cappuccino.
tldr: Cappuccino is a proven incomplete-dominant morph—two copies produce the super form called Frappuccino, not a non-viable animal.
Some breeders report reduced clutch sizes and developmental issues from Cappuccino × Cappuccino pairings, though this is not universally confirmed.
The 'scam' label is misdirected—super cappuccinos do exist and hatch, unlike Super Lilly White which is confirmed lethal.
The ethical case against Cap × Cap pairings rests on unresolved viability data, not on genetics being fake.
If you pursue Frappuccino, document every clutch outcome and share results—the community needs the data.

faq.question: Is super cappuccino (Frappuccino) a real crested gecko morph?
faq.answer: Yes. Cappuccino is a proven incomplete-dominant morph, and the homozygous super form — called Frappuccino — is real and has been hatched. It produces an amplified saddle pattern and a paler, more neutral body tone. The debate isn't about whether the morph exists; it's about whether the pairing required to produce it carries viability risks.

faq.question: Is breeding Cappuccino × Cappuccino dangerous?
faq.answer: Potentially. Some breeders report higher egg-failure rates and developmental concerns from Cap × Cap pairings, though this isn't systematically confirmed. It is NOT a confirmed lethal pairing like Lilly White × Lilly White. The conservative recommendation is to breed Cappuccino × non-carrier unless you're prepared to document every clutch outcome thoroughly.

faq.question: What is the difference between Cappuccino and Frappuccino?
faq.answer: Cappuccino is the single-copy (heterozygous) form: warm brown body, connected dorsal saddle, proven incomplete-dominant. Frappuccino is the two-copy (homozygous) super form: amplified saddle, paler body color, cleaner pattern boundaries. A Cappuccino × Cappuccino pairing should produce approximately 25% Frappuccino, 50% Cappuccino, and 25% non-carrier offspring.

faq.question: Why do people call super cappuccino a scam?
faq.answer: Critics argue that Cap × Cap pairings risk egg viability and are ethically questionable given uncertain data — not that the morph is genetically fake. The 'scam' label is misdirected. Frappuccinos exist, hatch, and live. The legitimate concern is whether the pairing strategy carries sublethal welfare costs, which is an ethical debate, not a fraud claim.

faq.question: Is Cappuccino the same inheritance type as Lilly White?
faq.answer: Both are incomplete-dominant morphs, meaning one copy is visual and two copies produce a super form. The difference is that Super Lilly White is a confirmed lethal allele — eggs fail to hatch. Frappuccino is not confirmed lethal; animals do hatch and survive. The viability concerns around Cap × Cap appear to be a different and less severe phenomenon.

faq.question: What pairing is safe for producing visual Cappuccino offspring?
faq.answer: Cappuccino × non-carrier is the safe standard pairing. It produces approximately 50% visual Cappuccino and 50% non-carrier offspring with no reported viability concerns. This is the recommended approach unless you're specifically pursuing Frappuccino and prepared to document outcomes carefully.

faq.question: How much does a Frappuccino crested gecko cost?
faq.answer: Pricing varies widely — typically anywhere from $1,500 to $3,500 or more, depending on the quality of the animal and whether the breeder can document the pairing and clutch outcomes. An undocumented Frappuccino should command less than one with a clear record of the pairing, hatch rate, and health history.

faq.question: Should I buy a Cappuccino crested gecko if there's controversy?
faq.answer: Yes — the controversy is specifically about Cap × Cap breeding, not about visual Cappuccinos themselves. Visual Cappuccinos from a Cappuccino × non-carrier pairing are uncontroversial, well-priced, and stack beautifully with other morphs. The morph is proven and legitimate. Just know the pairing history before buying.

angleIdeas: Breeders call super cappuccino 'a scam'—but the incomplete-dominant genetics say it should be viable. Here's what the community is actually afraid of.

The cappuccino controversy isn't really about the morph. It's about whether breeders should be chasing homozygous animals at all.
